summary: Made melee/gm/gmregcommon.c a matching linked object by adding the missing lbl_803D79F0 byte table, declaring it in the header, correcting several GALE01 data symbols to local @ labels, and switching gmregcommon from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The automated report credited one new match and showed main/melee/gm/gmregcommon .data reaching 100.00%.

key_files.role: Added the concrete u8 lbl_803D79F0 table needed for data matching.
Changed selected data labels from global lbl_* names to local @75/@76/@77 scoped symbols.
Promoted melee/gm/gmregcommon.c from NonMatching to Matching.

what_changed: configure.py now lists Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmregcommon.c") instead of Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmregcommon.c").
src/melee/gm/gmregcommon.c now defines lbl_803D79F0 as a 0x18-byte u8 array with values 0 through 17 plus 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, and 33.
src/melee/gm/gmregcommon.h now declares extern u8 lbl_803D79F0[].
config/GALE01/symbols.txt changes .data:0x803D7A08 and .data:0x803D7A40 from lbl_803D7A08/lbl_803D7A40 to local @75/@76 string symbols.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt changes .sdata:0x804D4050 from lbl_804D4050 to local @77.
decomp-dev reported Linked code +580 bytes, Matched data +96 bytes, Linked data +104 bytes, and one new match: main/melee/gm/gmregcommon .data from 85.37% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Kept the source change minimal: only the missing data table was added before promoting the object to Matching.
Fixed symbol scope in symbols.txt instead of leaving compiler-local strings as exported lbl_* globals.
Added the header declaration alongside the C definition, preventing the new data symbol from being hidden from other code that includes gmregcommon.h.
Used the build configuration change as the final linkage step after source and symbol-map data aligned.
decomp_lessons: Small data objects can be the last blocker for marking a translation unit as Matching; here a single lbl_803D79F0 byte array completed gmregcommon data matching.
symbols.txt scope is part of matching hygiene: local strings should use @NN names with scope:local when the object data is compiler-local.
When a decompiled data symbol remains semantically unknown, preserving the address-style lbl_803D79F0 name is acceptable evidence-grounded naming.
Promoting an object in configure.py should be paired with the source/data-symbol fixes that make the linked object actually match.
naming_conventions: Unidentified global data kept the address-derived lbl_803D79F0 name.
Compiler-local data labels in config/GALE01/symbols.txt were represented as @75, @76, and @77 with scope:local.
Existing gmregcommon function names were not renamed in this PR; the diff preserves names such as gm_8017BE84, gm_8017BE8C, and gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recovered raw .data as a C u8 array rather than using an assembly/data blob.
Matched symbol-map local scope by replacing global lbl_* string symbols with @NN local symbols.
Used configure.py Object(Matching) promotion to link the now-matching gmregcommon unit.
Automated decomp.dev metrics confirmed the tactic: gmregcommon .data reached 100.00%.
review_feedback: No PR body text and no inline review comments were present in the slice.
jurrejelle approved the PR without written review feedback.
decomp-dev bot validated the result with one new match and positive linked/matched byte deltas.
